results.intent: Reset the password for user 48213 to a temporary value since they got locked out after forgetting their login credentials
As part of our quarterly security policy, update employee jsmith's account password to comply with the new complexity requirements
results.raw_response:
{"selected_method": "PUT", "selected_path": "/users/{userId}/password", "parameters": {"userId": "smith"}}

Reasoning: The selected method is "PUT" as it allows updating an existing user's password. The selected path is "/users/{userId}/password" which corresponds to the userId parameter in the request. The parameters are provided as {userId: "smith"}, where "smith" should be replaced with the actual username or email address of the user whose password needs to be updated. This ensures that only the specified user's password is being modified according to the given requirement.

Can you pull up the connection details and current status for our production Postgres cluster with ID a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890? I need to check its configuration before we onboard a new microservice.

I need to compare bandwidth consumption across all our regional apps for March 15th to figure out which one caused the spike in our hosting bill.
We had a database failover in our production cluster—please reassign floating IP 203.0.113.42 to the standby droplet so traffic reroutes without changing DNS records.
Our droplet tied to floating IP 198.51.100.7 is being decommissioned tonight; unassign the IP from it first so we can attach it to the replacement server tomorrow.
Set up a new firewall for our production Kubernetes cluster that only allows inbound traffic on ports 80 and 443 from the load balancer subnet.
We're onboarding a new client environment and need a firewall created that restricts SSH access to just our office IP range and opens port 5432 for the database team's VPN.
results.raw_response: {"selected_method": "POST", "selected_path": "/v2/firewalls", "parameters": {"ip_ranges": ["192.168.0.0/24"], "port": 5432, "description": "Restricts SSH access to our office IP range and opens port 5432 for the database team's VPN."}}

parameters.description: Restricts SSH access to our office IP range and opens port 5432 for the database team's VPN.
